observations.recommendation:
1.7 In view of the noted lapse, we have recommended that-
 Management establish an Inventory Committee that will conduct
comprehensive annual physical count of PPE of the LGU;
 Inventory Committee prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) properly reconciled with
the PPE records of the GSO and Municipal Accountant, and submit
the same to COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year;
 Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer maintain PPE
Ledger Cards and Property Cards for easy reconciliation of the
account;
 General Services Officer maintain ARE/PAR and ensure its renewal
every three years; and
 Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjusting entries for
variances noted, if any, to establish the accuracy of the reported
balance of the PPE;

2.4 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that Management –
 Create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a yearly physical
count of local road network projects of the LGU;
 Instruct the General Services Officer to prepare a Report on the
Physical Count of the Local Road Network and submit the same to
COA for evaluation;
 Require the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road
Inventory and Road Map, update the same based on the results of the
annual physical count, and submit to COA for evaluation; and
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for
all local roads and reconcile the same with the result of the annual
physical count conducted.

3.8 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
 Analyze the transaction and adjust accordingly the books of accounts.
Require the Municipal Agriculture Office to submit the
acknowledgement receipt to support the dropping from the books of
accounts.
 Provide adequate disclosure on biological assets in the Notes to
Financial Statements, such as, but not limited to the following: (a)
basis of measurement or value recorded in the books; (b) description
of the biological assets, and (c) the program under which such
biological assets were purchased and its purpose;
 Maintain Ledger Card for the Breeding Stock; and
 Coordinate with the Municipal Agriculture Office for dispersal
program under their office, and require submission of duly
accomplished acknowledgement receipt for proper accounting of
animals in the books.

4.7 We have recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to come up with
accurate balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts of the
LGU, and henceforth, reclassify completed projects to their PPE accounts
once completed and accepter by the LGU.

Proposed Adjusting Entries (PAJEs):

5.3 We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor
to prepare and submit a copy of the Real Property Tax Assessment Roll to
the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to serve as their basis in
recording the RPT Receivable and Deferred RPT at the beginning of the
calendar year, and furnish this Office a copy of the Assessment Roll
for proper evaluation.

6.3 We have recommended that Management evaluate the purpose of the
funds as well as their implementations, and request for their proper
disposition, if necessary, in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-001.

7.5 We have recommended that Management:
 Discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advances despite
non-liquidation of previous ones;
 Facilitate the speedy liquidation of the cash advances and submit
Liquidation Reports to this Office for audit purposes; and
 Institute measures on the process of granting and liquidation of cash
advances by officials and employees.

8.3 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that Management
require the concerned officers/employees to provide the Municipal
Accountant with the necessary documents to support the Journal Entry
Vouchers made in the General Journal and submit the same to COA for
proper evaluation.

9.4 We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses and
require the Municipal Accountant to use the Cash Receipts Journal (CRJ)
in recording all collections of the LGU as required by the above-cited
provisions.

10.4 We have recommended that Acting Municipal Treasurer:
 Immediately deposit the undeposited collections to the LGU’s
authorized government depository bank and furnish this Office a copy
of the deposit slip; and
 Release the payroll to the claimants or authorized representatives
subject to proper documentation, and facilitate the liquidation of the
advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-
002.

11.6 We have recommended that Management instruct concerned officers to
submit/present the unpresented accountable forms to this office for audit.
Henceforth, ensure that all accountable forms and deposit slips are
included in the RAAFs and RCDs to arrive at accurate cash balances, as
well as, issue accountable forms in numerical sequence to easily monitor
the movement, status and accountability of the collecting officer on
accountable forms.

12.6 We have recommended that Management:
 Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the
account and prepare adjusting entries for the unreconciled accounts,
if necessary;
 Revisit the Memorandum of Agreement with the source agencies for
the utilized funds in the prior years and plan the implementation of
the projects specified, if still practicable;
 Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the BTr accounts of the
source agencies the unutilized portion of funds for the completed
projects, as well as the balance of the funds for projects which are
already impracticable;
 Adhere to the guidelines prescribed by COA Circular No. 94-013 on
the granting, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers.

13.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records, and
immediately remit noted discrepancies, if any. Henceforth, strictly comply
with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 8291.

14.6 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended that Management
remit all contributions and loan amortizations due to Pag-IBIG within the
reglementary period to avoid imposition of sanctions, fines and penalties.

15.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records, and
immediately remit any noted discrepancies based on the reconciliation.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the Implementing Rules and Regulations
of Republic Act No. 10606.

16.3 We have recommended that Management require the suppliers to replace
the collection receipts with sales invoices and/or official receipts to avoid
audit suspensions. Henceforth, ensure that all payments to suppliers be
supported with the principal receipts or invoices as enumerated in the
above-cited revenue regulation.

17.4 We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the MSWD
Officer on the lacking documents and submit the same for proper
evaluation on the entitlement of the recipients by the audit team.
Henceforth, Municipal Treasurer should ensure complete documentation
for every type of transaction, in accordance with applicable rules and
regulations, before effecting payment.

18.5 We have recommended that Management resort to direct payments to
suppliers and/or creditors, whenever possible, instead of procuring goods
and services through reimbursement basis to prevent the loss of
government funds and resources.

19.9 We have recommended that Management prepare the APP which would
serve as basis and control of procurement activities of the LGU and
instruct the Municipal Budget Officer maintain the required registries as
a monitoring tool of appropriations and obligations. Henceforth, follow
the approved annual budget and secure supplemental budget in case of
expenses not included in the approved original annual budget.

20.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer submit to COA the lacking reports of collections and
deposits for audit purposes. Henceforth, ensure the submission of accounts
and other financial and mandatory reports within the prescribed periods
pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160, Section 70 of NGAS for
LGUs, Item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-01 and Paragraph 7 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006.

21.5 We have recommended that Management submit to COA all perfected
contracts for the immediate review of the documents and, henceforth, be
guided on the submission of the same as prescribed by COA Circular No.
2009-001.

22.7 We have recommended that Management submit the duly approved
LSB/SEF Budget for CY 2021. Henceforth, ensure that Special Education
Fund Budget is prepared and approved by the Local School Board before
disbursing funds.

23.8 We have recommended that Management prepare and/or submit the
approved APP and its supporting PPMPs for audit purposes. Henceforth,
attach copies of approved APP and PPMPs to all procurement
transactions to serve as guide in the procurement activities of the LGU.

23.13 We have recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to cause the immediate submission of the eligibility
requirements of all bidders for every contract awarded for proper
evaluation by the audit team.

23.19 We have recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of the
Government Procurement Reform Act and its Implementing Rules and
Regulations.

23.24 We have recommended that Management ensure that invitations in
writing are sent to observers at least five (5) days before the conduct of
any procurement activity to enhance transparency in the procurement
process and attach the received invitation letters to the contract which will
form as evidence of invitation pursuant to the above-cited provisions.

24.5 We have recommended that Management strictly adhere to the
Government Procurement Reform Act and its Implementing Rules and
Regulations especially on the conduct of public bidding for transactions
exceeding the thresholds.

25.4 In view of the noted deficiency, we have recommended that Management
ensure the separate and proper allocation of the required funds for the
Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities in order to improve the well-
being of the elderly and the persons with disabilities. Henceforth, abide
with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-59 and
DBM-DSWD Circular No. 2003-01.

26.3 In view of the noted deficiency, we have recommended that Management
abide with the provisions of R.A. No. 9344 and ensure the proper
allocation of the required funds for the Local Council for the Protection of
Children in order to improve the well-being and cater the needs of the
children in the Municipality.

27.5 We have recommended that Management require the DRRM Officer to
prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
within the prescribed period for the timely evaluation of the transactions
charged to the fund. Henceforth, comply with the provisions of IRR of RA
10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

28.4 We have recommended that Management strictly comply with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the related subsequent
issuances on LDRRMF utilization. Henceforth, ensure that expenses
which are administrative in nature shall be sourced out from the General
Fund of the office.

29.6 We have recommended that Management submit the following documents
to COA for proper evaluation:
 For the welfare goods distributed to constituents, distribution
documents showing the names of the recipients, the items received,
quantity of the items received, name of the barangay, signature and
date of receipt of the items;
 For the medical supplies used by the RHU and frontliners, Report of
Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) supported with Requisition and
Issue Slip (RIS), as appropriate; and
 Photograph documentations of the COVID-19 response aid and
operations of the Municipality relative to the above expenditures,
properly captioned and dated.

30.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant cause the immediate transfer of the unutilized portion of the
QRF and DRRM-MOOE to the separate depository account of LDRRMF
in the Trust Fund books. As well as, the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the balances of the Cash in Bank, LCCA - LDRRMF and Trust
Liabilities – DRRMF and effect necessary adjusting entries to come up
with the correct balances of the accounts.

31.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the
required disclosures on DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements to
come up with more informative financial statements.

32.4 We have recommended the Management transfer the funds to its proper
account and ensure the implementation of the programs and projects
designed in conformity with the purposes of the received funds.
Henceforth, abide with the above-cited provisions especially on the
preparation and submission of quarterly reports on the fund utilization
and status of implementation of various programs/projects charged under
the fund

33.6 We have recommended that Management, henceforth, identify priority
projects to be funded under the 20% Development Fund and ensure the
implementation of PPAs included in the Annual Investment Plan to
achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes of the LGU.

34.4 We have recommended that Management submit the lacking documents
to support the disbursements, and henceforth, adhere to COA Circular No.
2012-001 for complete documentation of common government
transactions.

35.5 We have recommended that Management identify GAD PPAs through the
use of HGDG which would be the basis in the budget determination
depending on its gender responsiveness or attribute to GAD interventions.

36.7 In the light of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the
Management have a thorough plan in order to avoid such violation and
ensure that the budgeted PPAs are properly implemented to properly
address the gender issues and concerns of the locality.

37.3 We have recommended that Management submit the required
reports/documents to COA as support to the GAD Accomplishment
Report submitted.

38.6 We have recommended that Management:
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile their records on taxes withheld and remitted, and
immediately remit to BIR any noted unremitted taxes;
 Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust accordingly in the books
based on the results of the reconciliation; and
 Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the NIRC to avoid
penalties/sanctions imposed by the BIR.

We have recommended that management adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement and see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such public bidding.  Resort to alternative modes of procurement may only be resorted to as warranted by the prevailing circumstances.

Management comment:
Considering the urgency of work and repairs, time constraints, situational conditions geographically, procurement through shopping is the only applicable procurement.
Rejoinder:
Although shopping and small value procurement are alternative modes of procurement these also are accompanied with conditions and guidelines for application. The audit team reiterated that in order to resort to the said modes of procurement, the LGU must comply with the conditions and requirements and submit complete set of bidding and contract documents for proper review and evaluation.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Engineer and BAC secretariat see to it that disbursement vouchers for government contracts/procurements are duly and properly supported before payments are made pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445. Henceforth, ensure that all procurement will be done in accordance with the provisions of R.A. 9184.

We have recommended the submission of the two contracts which were not yet submitted, else, the corresponding payments made thereof will be suspended/disallowed in audit. Henceforth, all perfected contracts and purchase orders be submitted to COA within five days after perfection thereof for the immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, conduct of inspection and other necessary audit procedures.

We have recommended that management determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to comply with the required submission dates of accounts and reports. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the accounts and reports for audit and the Municipal Treasurer prepare Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements.

We have recommended that all procurements shall be meticulously planned and that the same shall be within the approved budget of the LGU.  Resort to the reimbursement method of procurement shall be used sparingly and only in cases of extreme emergency and some other unforeseen and unavoidable situations.
We reiterate our recommendation that management ensure proper and complete documentation of all their future procurements in accordance with the requirements of existing laws, rules and regulations.

Management comment:
Management justified that procurement were usually urgent and emergency in nature that need to address on-going project implementation.
Rejoinder:
The audit team reiterated that the disbursements discussed are not emergency – danger in life and property or unforeseeable contingencies since these are common government transactions which can be prepared/planned or anticipated.

We have recommended that the management discontinue the employment of private counsels/firms to handle legal cases of the municipality; otherwise it will be disallowed in audit.  In the event that the Provincial fiscal could not represent the LGU, a written concurrence from COA shall first be secured before such legal expenses may be validly paid.

Management comment:
Recommendation was noted. The management ensured the discontinuance of the payments for legal services to private counsels.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation, adoption and updating of the CLUP and CDP for CY 2018 onwards.

Management comment:
Management informed the audit team that the CLUP is on process of finalization.

We have recommended that management submit explanation on the noted lapses, else the same will be suspended/disallowed in audit. Henceforth, strictly implement PPAs in accordance with the Sanggunian Bayan approved appropriation ordinance for the municipality’s share from the tobacco excise tax.

Management comment:
The BAC and SB Resolutions for the realigned project were already submitted to COA.
Rejoinder:
The audit team requested for the submission of the additional supporting documents of the SB Resolution No. 48 series of 2016 for the change of program, such as but not limited to the ABC, detailed estimates, plans and elevations and the approval of the DBM for the change of project.

We have recommended that a special book of account and depository account be maintained solely for the share from Tobacco Excise Tax (RA 7171) to facilitate monitoring and better internal control over the fund.

We have reiterated that the municipality maintain a special account with complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund to facilitate monitoring of development projects and balances. Ensure that operations of the fund be accounted for through the maintenance of books of accounts and complete subsidiary ledgers to enable the generation of financial statements at any given time.

Management comment:
Soft copy of the subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund was maintained, but no copy was submitted to COA. Separate financial statement for the 20% DF will be prepared for CY 2018.

We have reiterated the necessity to maintain a special account and complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund to facilitate monitoring of development projects. We have recommended that management through the MPDC formulate the AIP in accordance with the identified significant needs of the people in the locality. We have also recommended that the Municipal Engineer monitor the status of the unimplemented projects in CY 2016, specifically those that were already awarded to different contractors.  Henceforth, ensure maximum utilization of the fund for development projects.

Management comment:
The PRDP and KALAHI-CIDDS projects which were with local counterparts did not prosper, thus the unutilized balances, but were already realigned to different projects.

In view of the noted observations, we have recommended the following:

the MPDC lead the setting up and maintenance of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;
the GAD GFPS prepare the GPB through the application of the HGDG and submit the same to the PPDO for DILG review and approval; and
that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Point to identify programs, projects and activities that are gender-responsive such as promotion of gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfillment of women’s human rights and promotion of women’s economic empowerment to be included in the GAD Plan.

Management comment:
The LGU submitted a certification from the DILG Provincial Officer of the approval of the GAD plan and budget for the CY 2017. The management will request for the DILG to orient the GFPS the use of the HDGP, while GAD database are maintained manually with still limited information.
Rejoinder:
The audit team received the DILG certification after the exit conference conducted on October 3, 2018. It was noted that as per pertinent section of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, the approval of the GPBs is by the DILG Regional Office.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the management ensure that the LDRRMF, specifically the 70% mitigation fund be used to support disaster risk management programs and activities; require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to the special bank account.

Management comment:
The management agreed to comply on the audit recommendations for the succeeding transactions.

We have reiterated that management exhaust all available remedies provided by law and regulations in enforcing the immediate settlement of the outstanding audit suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:
53
a) the Municipal Treasurer record the verified reconciling items from
previous years;
b) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer trace back
transactions of prior periods to account for the un-reconciled difference
in the trust fund books;
c) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile monthly
the accounting with the treasury and the bank records to immediately
detect errors and ensure the correctness and accuracy of their records.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere to laws, rules and
regulations on the grant, utilization and settlement of cash advances. The
Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Treasurer to suspend the
payment of salaries of the officials and employees concerned for the restitution
of unliquidated cash advances. No employee shall be cleared from money and
property accountability unless their corresponding unliquidated cash advances
are first settled.

We have recommended that management implement the following measures to
ensure the reliability of the balances of PPE accounts and records:
 Physical inventory of fixed assets shall be made at least once a year;
 the Inventory Committee prepare the Physical Inventory Reports to be
submitted to the Property Officer for verification and reconciliation with
the records maintained by the Accounting Office and the property cards of
the Property Unit;
 Establishment of property identification and coding;
 Maintenance of the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment to establish
the official accountable for the property;
 Reclassification of unserviceable property noted during the physical count
of inventory to Other Assets and conduct a proper disposal of
unserviceable property; and
 PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards should be maintained and updated
by the respective offices in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGU.

We have recommended that:
 management promptly conduct an inventory of its road network system
and the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and
keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components;
 the Municipal Accountant trace the recorded road, highways and bridges
which were closed to government equity prior to 2014 and subsequently
record in its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to
the required transition provision of 25% for CY 2016 until it is 100% by
CY 2019 and keep and maintain Registry of Public Infrastructures for
Road and subsidiary records for roads and road components for every
road network; and
 the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO
with the complete description and cost segregation of road components
for road projects.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer trace back the source of the abnormal balances and record the
necessary adjustments to present fairly the financial statements of the
Municipality.

We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain complete
and updated subsidiary ledgers for all payables to avoid over and under
remittances. All trust collections should be promptly remitted to the
concerned agencies to avoid imposition of penalties and inconveniences to
employee-members.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer trace back the source of the unremitted amount and if verified as
such, the Municipal Treasurer should remit immediately the amount due to
PHILHEALTH. Remittances of trust liabilities should take priority over the
payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its
employees.

We recommend again that management submit SARO, Memorandum of Agreement
and other pertinent documents to support the funds received from other agencies
to determine the specific purpose for which they were granted in accordance with
pertinent sections of the NGAS Manual.

The Municipal Accountant should evaluate the previous years’ disbursements to
clarify whether the above-stated funds were already utilized but erroneously
recorded and adjust the accounting records. Likewise, liquidation papers
should be submitted in order to determine which of the funds received were
being liquidated.

We have reiterated to recommend that the Municipal Assessor submit the Real
Property Tax Assessment to the Municipal Treasurer to serve as their basis in
recording the RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year in accordance
with the provisions of the NGAS Manual and RA No. 7160.

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
1) that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Engineer to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers for
government infrastructure projects are duly and properly supported
before payments are made;
2) that the municipality consider the procurement of repairs and
maintenance on equipment through competitive bidding;
3) that management submit the lacking supporting documents; and
4) henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensures that all disbursements are
with complete supporting documents and submitted within the
reglementary period.

We have recommended that all perfected contracts and purchase orders be
submitted to COA within five days upon perfection for the immediate review
of the reasonableness of the cost and in order to facilitate the conduct of
inspection.

Henceforth, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal
of submitted vouchers to the Municipal Accountant, who after recording in the
Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the timely
conduct of audit and early communication of noted deficiencies so as to
improve financial operations of the Municipal Government. This way,
management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the
causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports.

We have recommended that all Memorandum of Agreements entered into by
the municipality and Notice of Authority to Debit Account Issued be submitted
to the audit team for verification. Henceforth, ensure that funds received from
National Government Agencies and all other sources are covered by
Memorandum of Agreement and that the same shall be submitted to COA
within 5 days from perfection thereof for audit purposes.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the municipal
officials concerned to submit the required accomplishment reports and list of
all PPAs with complete information, regardless of source.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:
 the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to prepare the
ABC for each infrastructure project of the Municipality;
 the Municipal Mayor instruct the department heads to prepare their
PPMP to be consolidated by the BAC Secretariat into the APP and the
Municipal Budget Officer should ensure that the APP is consistent with
the duly approved yearly budget;
 the BAC observe the required pre-procurement conference for
infrastructure project with an ABC above ₱5,000,000.00;
 the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat observe the proper advertising and
posting of the Invitation to Bid as prescribed in the Revised IRR of R.A.
9184 to encourage more participants in the bidding process and intensify
the competition among them, which in turn, will ensure that the LGU will
get the best possible proposals, as to quality and cost, for the project
sought to be procured;
 the BAC observe the bidding process and prepare the necessary records
and reports as support to the contract and bidding documents;
 the BAC invite in writing observers to observe in the procurement
activities of the Municipality;
 the members of the BAC submit justification/ explanation on the noted
violations and ensure that prospective bidders submit complete eligibility
requirements and post the necessary bid security before they are marked
as “passed” and declared as LCB and require the prospective bidders
post the required performance security prior to contract signing;
 the BAC secretariat submit the noted bidding documents; and
 the BAC members should attend trainings on updates on the different
provisions of the Government Procurement Law or R.A. 9184.

We have recommended that management ensure that:
 the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer see to it that
Disbursement Vouchers for government contracts are duly and properly
supported before payment pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445;
 adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement and shall see to
it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such
public bidding.

We have recommended the following:
• That the Municipality determine the cost of the unperformed portion of
the works for every day of delay and compute the amount of liquidated
damages in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. 9184 and the
contract agreement; and
• that management enforce immediate collection of liquidated damages and
ensure the refund of the excess of the bill of quantities from the contract
price from the contractors.
